channel.title: CyberNetSec - Ransomware
channel.description: Latest ransomware threat intelligence - the last 10 articles

item.title: Ransomware Negotiator Admits to Conspiring with BlackCat Gang
item.description:
Angelo Martino, a former ransomware negotiator, has pleaded guilty to conspiring with the notorious BlackCat (ALPHV) ransomware gang. Martino abused his position at a crypto brokerage firm, using his insider knowledge of his clients' negotiating strategies and insurance limits to help BlackCat maxim...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Ransomware, Policy and Compliance

Read Full Article →

item.title: Ransomware Turf War: 0APT and KryBit Groups Hack Each Other in Public Feud, Leaking Ops Data
item.description:
A rare public conflict has erupted between two rival ransomware groups, 0APT and KryBit. The feud, which played out between March 28 and April 12, 2026, involved both groups hacking each other and leaking sensitive operational data. 0APT initiated the spat by leaking KryBit's admin panel. KryBit ret...

Severity: Low

Categories: Threat Actor, Ransomware

Read Full Article →

item.title: Medtronic Confirms Data Breach After ShinyHunters Claims Theft of 9 Million Records and Corporate Data
item.description:
Medical device manufacturer Medtronic has confirmed it sustained a cybersecurity incident following claims by the ShinyHunters threat group. The group alleged it stole over 9 million records and terabytes of corporate data, issuing a 'pay or leak' ultimatum. Medtronic stated the breach was contained...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Threat Actor, Ransomware

Read Full Article →

item.title: Europol IOCTA Report: AI, Encryption, and Data Theft are Fueling an Industrialized Cybercrime Wave
item.description:
Europol's 2026 Internet Organised Crime Threat Assessment (IOCTA) report warns of an industrialized cybercrime landscape where AI, encryption, and proxies are making attacks faster and more sophisticated. The report highlights a significant shift in ransomware tactics towards data theft and extortio...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Policy and Compliance, Ransomware

Read Full Article →

item.title: VECT 2.0 Ransomware Flaw Means Paying the Ransom is Pointless—Large Files are Wiped Forever
item.description:
Security researchers at Check Point have uncovered a critical design flaw in the VECT 2.0 ransomware that causes it to permanently destroy files larger than 128 KB instead of encrypting them. This bug, present in the Windows, Linux, and ESXi variants, discards essential decryption keys (nonces) duri...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Malware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: ShinyHunters Ransomware Spree: Carnival, Zara's Parent, and 40+ Firms Breached in Massive Campaign
item.description:
The ShinyHunters ransomware group has claimed responsibility for a large-scale attack campaign that has compromised more than 40 organizations worldwide. The victims, listed on the group's data leak site, span the retail, insurance, and hospitality sectors. High-profile targets include Carnival Corp...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Data Breach, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: Wisconsin Hospital Mile Bluff Medical Center Hit by Ransomware, Enters Downtime Procedures
item.description:
Mile Bluff Medical Center in Mauston, Wisconsin, has confirmed it is recovering from a ransomware attack that occurred in April 2026. The attack encrypted files on the medical center's network, causing temporary disruptions to computer and phone systems. Clinical teams were forced to revert to estab...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Data Breach, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Trigona Ransomware Evolves, Using Custom Exfiltration Tool for Stealthier Data Theft
item.description:
Affiliates of the Trigona ransomware group are increasing their sophistication by using a custom-built data exfiltration tool named 'uploader_client.exe'. This move, identified by Symantec's Threat Hunter Team, marks a tactical shift from using common, easily-detected utilities like Rclone. The new ...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Malware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: US Treasury Sanctions Crypto Exchange and Mixers for Laundering Ransomware Proceeds
item.description:
The U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) has taken decisive action against the financial infrastructure supporting cybercrime by sanctioning a virtual currency exchange, 'Cortexchange', and two cryptocurrency mixing services, 'ChainScrub' and 'HelixMix'. These en...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Ransomware

Read Full Article →

item.title: Qilin Ransomware Group Targets City of Napoleon, Ohio, Threatening Municipal Data Leak
item.description:
The City of Napoleon, Ohio, has become the latest government entity to be targeted by the Qilin ransomware group. The group claimed responsibility for the cyberattack on April 23, 2026, adding the city to its data leak site. Qilin is employing a double extortion tactic, threatening to publish sensit...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Cyberattack, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →
